title: Indian People and Personalities
description: A round on notable Indians from science, literature, public life, reform, arts, and sport.

questions.prompt: Which Indian physicist won the Nobel Prize in 1930 for work on the scattering of light?
questions.hint: The effect is named after him.

questions.prompt: Who founded the Tata group and is often called the Father of Indian Industry?
questions.hint: His first name was Jamsetji.

questions.prompt: Which social reformer and educator founded the first school for girls in Pune with her husband Jyotirao?
questions.hint: She is a pioneering symbol of women's education in India.

questions.prompt: Who wrote the novel Godan?
questions.hint: He is one of the greatest Hindi-Urdu fiction writers.

questions.prompt: Which Indian aerospace scientist later became the 11th President of India?
questions.hint: He is called the Missile Man of India.

questions.prompt: Which musician from Benaras is credited with bringing the shehnai to the classical concert stage?
questions.hint: He was a Bharat Ratna awardee.

questions.prompt: Who founded the Self-Respect Movement in Tamil Nadu?
questions.hint: He is widely referred to simply by an honorific title meaning the Great One.

questions.prompt: Which mathematician from Tamil Nadu collaborated with G. H. Hardy at Cambridge?
questions.hint: He left behind famous notebooks.

questions.prompt: Which Indian economist won the Nobel Memorial Prize in 1998 and wrote Development as Freedom?
questions.hint: He has taught at Harvard and Oxford.

questions.prompt: Who was India's first woman Prime Minister?
questions.hint: She was the daughter of Jawaharlal Nehru.

questions.prompt: Which freedom fighter, poet, and political leader was known as the Nightingale of India?
questions.hint: She later became the first woman governor of an Indian state.

questions.prompt: Which filmmaker and writer created Feluda and Professor Shonku?
questions.hint: He directed Pather Panchali.

questions.prompt: Who founded the Indian Space Research Organisation's precursor INCOSPAR and is regarded as the father of the Indian space programme?
questions.hint: The space centre at Thumba is strongly associated with him.

questions.prompt: Which lawyer and nationalist was popularly called Deshbandhu?
questions.hint: He was a mentor to Subhas Chandra Bose.

questions.prompt: Which Indian classical vocalist of the Kirana gharana was known as Bharat Ratna Pandit Bhimsen?
questions.hint: His surname is Joshi.

questions.prompt: Who pioneered the White Revolution in India and is known as the Father of the White Revolution?
questions.hint: He was central to Amul and Operation Flood.

questions.prompt: Which Indian diplomat became the first woman president of the UN General Assembly in 1953?
questions.hint: She was the sister of Jawaharlal Nehru.

questions.prompt: Which Indian philosopher became the second President of India and is commemorated each year on Teachers' Day?
questions.hint: His initials are S. R.

questions.prompt: Who wrote the Bengali novel Pather Panchali?
questions.hint: He also wrote Aparajito.

questions.prompt: Which athlete became the first Indian to win an individual Olympic gold medal?
questions.hint: He won in shooting in Beijing 2008.

questions.prompt: Which 19th-century monk carried Vedanta to the Parliament of Religions in Chicago in 1893?
questions.hint: He was the chief disciple of Ramakrishna.

questions.prompt: Which Indian woman boxer from Manipur is nicknamed Magnificent Mary?
questions.hint: She is a multiple-time world champion.

questions.prompt: Which economist and policymaker served as the architect of India's 1991 economic reforms as finance minister?
questions.hint: He later became Prime Minister.

questions.prompt: Who founded the newspaper Kesari and the Deccan Education Society?
questions.hint: He gave the slogan 'Swaraj is my birthright'.

questions.prompt: Which dancer and choreographer was central to the revival of Bharatanatyam through Kalakshetra?
questions.hint: Her first name was Rukmini.

questions.prompt: Which scientist led India's Pokhran-II nuclear tests as scientific adviser and DRDO chief?
questions.hint: He later became President.

questions.prompt: Which modern Indian painter is famous for the Bharat Mata image and co-founding the Bengal School?
questions.hint: He was the nephew of Rabindranath Tagore.

questions.prompt: Who authored the Indian English novel Train to Pakistan?
questions.hint: He was also a noted columnist and historian.

questions.prompt: Which Indian industrialist founded Infosys along with six co-founders and later served as chairman?
questions.hint: His initials are N. R. N.

questions.prompt: Which freedom fighter and Gandhian is popularly remembered as Frontier Gandhi?
questions.hint: He was from the North-West Frontier region.

questions.prompt: Which dancer and model founded the Nrityagram dance village near Bengaluru?
questions.hint: Her first name was Protima.

questions.prompt: Which nationalist leader and economic thinker was called the Grand Old Man of India?
questions.hint: He was among the early presidents of the Indian National Congress.

questions.prompt: Which Indian-origin writer won the Booker Prize for The God of Small Things?
questions.hint: Her surname is Roy.

questions.prompt: Who founded the Bhakti movement-inspired Lingayat social-religious reform current in Karnataka?
questions.hint: He composed vachanas.

questions.prompt: Which Indian woman became the first to win a world title in badminton from India by claiming the BWF World Championships in 2019?
questions.hint: She also won Olympic medals in Rio and Tokyo.

questions.prompt: Which philosopher-sage of ancient India is credited with the Yoga Sutras?
questions.hint: His name is also associated with a Sanskrit grammatical tradition.

questions.prompt: Who wrote the nationalist song 'Sare Jahan Se Achha'?
questions.hint: His philosophical poetry later influenced beyond India too.

questions.prompt: Which Indian environmental activist led the Narmada Bachao Andolan?
questions.hint: Her first name is Medha.

questions.prompt: Which literary giant won the Nobel Prize in Literature in 1913 for Gitanjali?
questions.hint: He composed Jana Gana Mana.

questions.prompt: Which Indian military leader was the first Chief of Defence Staff of India?
questions.hint: He died in a helicopter crash in 2021.

questions.prompt: Who founded the newspaper Al-Hilal and became one of independent India's leading education ministers?
questions.hint: He is remembered as Maulana.

questions.prompt: Which playback singer from Karnataka is known for an unmatched range across Indian languages and won the most National Film Awards for singing?
questions.hint: His initials were S. P. B.

questions.prompt: Which scientist is known as the father of India's nuclear programme and founded the Tata Institute of Fundamental Research?
questions.hint: His surname is Bhabha.

questions.prompt: Which woman freedom fighter is closely associated with hoisting the Indian National Congress flag during the Quit India Movement in 1942?
questions.hint: Her surname is Asaf Ali.

questions.prompt: Which Indian jurist chaired the Drafting Committee of the Constitution?
questions.hint: He also served as independent India's first Law Minister.

questions.prompt: Who is the author of the Malayalam novel Chemmeen?
questions.hint: He won the Jnanpith Award in 1984.

questions.prompt: Which Indian woman mountaineer became the first from the country to climb Mount Everest in 1984?
questions.hint: Her first name is Bachendri.

questions.prompt: Which social activist is popularly known as the Waterman of India?
questions.hint: He worked extensively in Rajasthan on johads and water conservation.

questions.prompt: Which constitutional expert and statesman served as the first Indian Governor-General after Lord Mountbatten?
questions.hint: He was popularly called Rajaji.

questions.prompt: Which Indian captain led the hockey team to Olympic golds in 1928, 1932, and 1936 and is called the Wizard of Hockey?
questions.hint: His first name was Dhyan.
